frontier.hypothesis: F6 (from prior brief, unmeasured): SOTN-family carve-out application - duplicated-statement-into-arms and dead-store-fake-exception on arg5's carrier to lift pri(arg5) >= pri(t0) via reg_n_refs, potentially flipping the h5 basin's residual pair-swap through the qty_compare priority equation.
frontier.mechanism: s69 forensics CONFIRMED the debug_printf window pseudos allocate identically across prologue-shape perturbations - therefore the pair-swap residual is NOT touchable via prologue levers. F6's proposed vehicle (reg_n_refs lift on arg5's carrier via SOTN-sanctioned duplication into do_timeout arms) targets the block-3 window directly, orthogonal to the exhausted prologue-lever axis.
frontier.next_probe: Enumerate do_timeout arrival arms in src/system.c; identify a real statement referencing arg5 or its carrier that can be duplicated into BOTH arms with cross-jump byte-neutrality; measure via sandbox --disable all; if byte-neutral AND lifts pri(arg5) >= pri(t0), invoke layer-2 cheat-reviewer per no-new-park-categories prerequisites (FAKE annotation + lever-exhaustion + independent verdict). Judge constraint: this is the ONLY unexhausted axis for the h5 basin residual under current policy.

frontier.hypothesis: F9 (novel this session, low probability): The 4-cycle s-reg rotation basin coupled through qty_compare is a hint - if a C construct can force pri(p72) = pri(p79) EXACTLY (a strict tie), MIPS's ascending-regno find_reg would break the tie by ord= sequence rather than by pri delta, potentially yielding a DIFFERENT rotation basin than either of the two measured here.
frontier.mechanism: qty_compare tie-breaking priority: when floor_log2(refs)*refs/livelen*10000 evaluates equal for two competing carriers, the tie is broken by insertion order (RTL first-set LUID). If we can seed idx_1494 and tbl_125c with identical (refs, livelen) via a byte-neutral C shape (e.g., a shared prologue helper), the tie would resolve differently.
frontier.next_probe: Enumerate prologue shapes that give tbl_125c and idx_1494 carriers identical (refs=3, livelen=~6) at the pre-loop insertion point. Candidate: swap the declaration/initialization order at lines 404-406 while preserving byte-equivalent RTL first-uses. Measure both directions (swap lines 404/405, then swap 405/406). If any variant produces a 3rd rotation basin distinct from h5 and honest, we have a new axis of exploration.

frontier.hypothesis: F10 (novel this session): The 8-insn prologue-length delta between h5 (43 insns) and honest (35 insns) may include a scheduler-relevant NOTE_INSN_LOOP_BEG boundary that changes birthing_insn_p classification for later insns beyond just the s-reg rotation.
frontier.mechanism: SCHEDDBG line-counts are IDENTICAL (10626 each), so the block-3 debug_printf window's scheduling proceeds identically. But the 8-insn prologue shift may affect the number of LUIDs before block 3 begins, offsetting all subsequent LUIDs by ~8. This does NOT affect ready-queue LUID tie-breaks WITHIN a block (LUID diffs are preserved), so is unlikely to help - but a directed sched2 dump comparison could confirm.
frontier.next_probe: Grep sched2.csmd4 for block=3 dispatch order in both dumps and compare - if identical (as expected), close F10 empirically.
